hero.title: Welcome to My Digital Rebellion
hero.subtitle: Fuck the corporate cloud. Build your own.
hero.description: I'm a self-hoster who's done with watching Big Tech monetize every keystroke, harvest every click, and lock you into ecosystems designed to extract maximum profit from your digital life. This is my war room where I build tools, share tactics, and forge weapons for anyone ready to tear down the walled gardens and reclaim what's rightfully theirs: complete control over your computing.

sections.title: Manifesto
sections.content: The internet is dying under the boot of surveillance capitalism. Five corporations own your digital existence. They've turned "the cloud" into a euphemism for "rent-seeking middleman" and "user-friendly" into code for "we control everything."

list.title: Self-hosting everything
list.text: Your email, your repos, your files, your life. If you're paying monthly rent for storage that sits on someone else's hardware, you're a serf in digital feudalism.

list.title: Building with open source exclusively
list.text: Proprietary software is a cage. The source code is the key. Anyone telling you otherwise profits from your captivity.

list.title: Burning down surveillance capitalism
list.text: Every "free" service is a panopticon. Stop volunteering for your own surveillance.

list.title: Weaponizing self-reliance
list.text: Learn to run your own infrastructure or accept that you'll always be at the mercy of some corporation's quarterly earnings call.

sections.quote: The cloud isn't the future. It's a scam. Host your own goddamn servers.

items.name: Git (Forgejo)
items.function: Self-hosted git server. The primary forge for my software and tactics.

items.name: Cloud (Nextcloud)
items.function: My digital safe haven. Files, contacts, and calendar without corporate harvesting.

items.name: Jellyfin
items.function: Media liberation. Ripping, storing, and managing my own media collection.

items.name: Immich
items.function: High-performance photo backup. Reclaiming my visual history from Google Photos.

items.name: Authelia
items.function: Single Sign-On and 2FA. Securing the perimeter of my entire network.

items.name: Vaultwarden
items.function: Secure password management. Keeping my keys out of corporate hands.

items.name: are
items.description: Another Rust Editor - A port of AEE (Another Easy Editor) to rust.

sections.title: Join the Resistance
sections.content: I'm building networks with developers, sysadmins, and digital freedom fighters who are tired of asking permission to use their own fucking computers. reach out via email or through my repositories.
